Comparison

SpiniX vs Kangaroo Rewards

Kangaroo is a strong mid-market loyalty platform built around POS integration and app-based engagement. SpiniX is a hospitality-first platform built around QR gamification and Wallet passes. Different tools for different problems.

SpiniX vs Kangaroo Rewards: the short version

SpiniX vs Kangaroo Rewards: feature comparison

Kangaroo RewardsSpiniX
Starting price €29/mo
POS integration required No - standalone QR
Guest enrollment method QR scan - 15 seconds, no app
Apple/Google Wallet Native - direct to Wallet
Gamification Spin wheel on every QR scan
Reward types Instant spin rewards every visit
Google review generation Automatic flow (33% conversion)
Email marketing Automated 6-email sequence
SMS marketing WhatsApp (SEA/UK markets)
Referral program Not yet (planned)
Tiered loyalty No - instant rewards model
Purchase tracking Visit-based (scan frequency)
Setup time 10 minutes (print QR, done)
E-commerce support No (in-person only)
White-label app No
Multi-location Yes - per location

SpiniX vs Kangaroo Rewards pricing

SpiniX

Starter €29/mo
  • • 1 spin wheel
  • • QR + Wallet
  • • Email automation
  • • Review generation
Growth €59/mo
  • • 3 wheels
  • • Priority support
  • • Advanced analytics
Pro €99/mo
  • • Unlimited wheels
  • • Multi-location
  • • WhatsApp integration

No POS needed. No setup fees. Wallet passes billed per install ($0.15).

Kangaroo Rewards

Starter $79/mo
  • • Core loyalty features
  • • Basic integration
  • • Points & rewards
Plus Custom quote
  • • Advanced segmentation
  • • White-label app
  • • Multi-location
Enterprise Custom quote
  • • API access
  • • AI campaigns
  • • Full customization

POS integration may have additional setup costs depending on provider.

SpiniX vs Kangaroo Rewards: where each wins

Kangaroo wins: deep purchase data

Because Kangaroo integrates with your POS, it knows exactly what each customer bought, how much they spent, and when. This powers advanced segmentation - you can target "customers who spent $50+ in the last 30 days" or "people who bought menu item X." SpiniX tracks visits and engagement but not individual purchases. If purchase-level data drives your marketing strategy, Kangaroo has the edge.

Kangaroo wins: referral and tiered loyalty

Kangaroo offers built-in referral programs (reward both referrer and new customer) and tiered loyalty (Bronze, Silver, Gold). These are powerful for businesses with high-frequency repeat customers. SpiniX uses instant rewards on every visit instead of tiers, which drives different psychology - immediate gratification vs aspirational progression.

SpiniX wins: zero-friction enrollment

Kangaroo requires either an app download or registration at the POS checkout. Both create friction. SpiniX: guest scans QR at the table, enters email, spins, wins. 15 seconds, no app, no POS interaction, no staff involvement. This is why QR-based loyalty achieves 46% enrollment vs 12% for app-based programs.

SpiniX wins: gamification that works on every phone

Kangaroo offers "Spin to Win" but only on a tablet at the counter. The guest has to go to the tablet and interact with it. SpiniX delivers the spin wheel directly to the guest's phone via QR - every table, every guest, no hardware. Variable rewards on every scan produce 3x more dopamine than deterministic point systems.

SpiniX wins: automatic Google review generation

After a guest wins a reward, SpiniX shows an optional Google review prompt. 33% leave a review. That's built into the flow, not a separate tool. Kangaroo doesn't include review generation. You'd need a separate service ($50-200/mo) to match what SpiniX includes by default.

SpiniX wins: no POS dependency

Kangaroo works best when connected to Shopify, Lightspeed, Square, or similar POS systems. No POS? Limited functionality. SpiniX works with any business regardless of POS - print a QR code, stick it on the table, done. This makes SpiniX ideal for restaurants using basic POS systems, cash registers, or no POS at all.

SpiniX vs Kangaroo Rewards: FAQ

Can I switch from Kangaroo to SpiniX?

Yes. SpiniX is standalone, so you can run both simultaneously during transition. Export your customer emails from Kangaroo and import them into SpiniX. The QR-based enrollment will capture new guests immediately.

Does Kangaroo work without a POS?

Technically yes, through their tablet or manual entry. But Kangaroo's core strength is POS integration. Without it, you lose purchase tracking, automatic point awarding, and checkout-based enrollment - which are the main reasons to choose Kangaroo.

Why doesn't SpiniX have a referral program?

It's on the roadmap. Currently, SpiniX focuses on in-venue engagement (QR scan at the table). Referrals are a different acquisition channel. If referrals are critical to your strategy right now, Kangaroo or a dedicated referral tool may be better.

Which has better analytics?

Different strengths. Kangaroo shows purchase-level data (what was bought, how much spent). SpiniX shows engagement data (scan rate, spin rate, email open rate, review conversion, return visits). For hospitality, SpiniX's metrics are more actionable.

Try SpiniX free for 14 days

No POS integration needed. No app for your guests to download. Set up in 10 minutes, see results the same week.
